notes: First-party data. Dollar figures rounded per stated policy (nearest $50 above $500, toward the less flattering direction for revenue). Client and channel names redacted by policy. Unmarked figures are records, not estimates.

ventures.label: VerifiedDesk services desk

revenue.concentration: no single client exceeds half of total

ventures.verdict: fastest path to first revenue in portfolio

ventures.label: V2 content network
ventures.verdict: compounds but platform-taxed and hit-driven; survivor of multiple attempts

ventures.label: V3 trading research
ventures.verdict: research program, not income, until live records exist

ventures.label: sports documentary channel
ventures.post_mortem: structural copyright exposure: core input was third-party IP
ventures.verdict: if the core input is someone else's IP you have a countdown, not a business

ventures.label: faith-content brand
ventures.post_mortem: niche monetization per view could not support even an automated cost base
ventures.verdict: automation cannot rescue underwater unit economics

ventures.label: AI voice-agent agency
ventures.model: agency reselling AI receptionists to local businesses
ventures.status: declined after adversarial research, never launched
ventures.post_mortem: smallest-business demand is push not pull (adoption under 20 percent and flat); squeezed by $49-79/mo self-serve and vertical incumbents; guru retainer pricing and channel claims failed source verification
ventures.verdict: declining a business is also a data point
